channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The Super Bowl was made for TV and instant replay was made for visual AI. Here’s how it could be better and what it would look like
item.description: Visual AI can make the big game — and every Sunday — better.

item.title: Ball is in the GOP's court on DHS funding standoff, House minority leader says
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries says he's not confident Congress will strike a deal to keep the Department of Homeland Security funded and it's ...

item.title: We Need a Private-Sector Overhaul of U.S. Space Exploration
item.description: The scrubbed Artemis launch is the latest saga of an inefficient, unfocused government-run space program.

item.title: West Virginia worked with ICE — 650 arrests later, officials say Minnesota-style 'chaos' is a choice
item.description: ICE surge in West Virginia nets 650 illegal immigrants in two weeks with strong local cooperation, contrasting sharply with Minnesota-style protests.

item.title: Goodbye, Kostyantynivka
item.description: Not too long ago, the eastern Ukrainian city helped build my family. Now, the town is little more than ruins.

item.title: Defiant Iran says missile program non-negotiable after meeting with U.S.
item.description: Iran's ballistic missile program was never on the table and was not up for negotiation during Friday's high-stakes meeting between Iranian and U.S. of...

item.title: At Last, a Positive Trend in American Education
item.description: Good news from our classrooms: More and more young people are learning how to manage their money.

item.title: CNN’s Love-Hate Relationship with Citizen Journalists in Minnesota
item.description: How the network feels toward them depends, of course, on what is being investigated.

item.title: LA city councilwoman previously backed by DSA running for mayor in primary challenge to former ally Bass
item.description: Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman launched a last-minute mayoral challenge against incumbent Karen Bass just hours before the filing deadli...

item.title: Thirty Candles for the Internet’s Foundation
item.description: Internet free speech is celebrating its 30th birthday — if Section 230’s opponents don’t spoil the party, that is.

item.title: Russian attacks on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure are the biggest threat to its economy, which could shrink as much as 3%
item.description: The cost of purchasing and operating generators to overcome power outages is just one of many challenges facing Ukrainian businesses after nearly four...

item.title: Super Bowl Sunday: Here are some of the political, social commercials you can expect during the big game
item.description: Super Bowl ads are about more than just products sometimes, with spots at this year's game highlighting Trump Accounts, antisemitism, faith and campai...

item.title: The single crushing problem American cattle ranchers wish Trump would fix instead
item.description: Trump expands beef imports to cut prices, but American cattle ranchers say the real problem squeezing their profits lies elsewhere.

item.title: Washington Post publisher to step down after big layoffs as union calls his legacy ‘attempted destruction of a great American journalism institution’
item.description: His tenure has been rocky from the start, marked by layoffs and a failed reorganization plan that led to the departure of former top editor Sally Buzb...

item.title: All the things you wanted to know about Super Bowl rings but were afraid to ask
item.description: One year's bling cost an estimated $36,500 each.

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds Trump mass detention policy for illegal immigrants
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled that Homeland Security can lawfully detain illegal immigrants without bond hearings, a major legal win for the Trump adm...

item.title: Minnesotans say immigration agents are impersonating construction workers, delivery drivers and anti-ICE activists
item.description: Local officials also say ICE agents have been seen swapping license plates or using bogus ones, a violation of state law.

item.title: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi hopes for big win as polls open in national elections
item.description: Polls opened Sunday in parliamentary elections that Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi hopes will give her struggling party a big enough win to pu...

item.title: Families of Venezuelans detained for political activism demand their release outside infamous prison
item.description: Dozens of relatives and friends of Venezuelan opposition leaders, human rights defenders and others detained for their political activities protested ...

item.title: Police arrest protesters at Minneapolis federal building on 1-month anniversary of woman's death
item.description: Police arrested at least several demonstrators Saturday outside a federal building in Minneapolis, breaking up a protest marking the one-month anniver...

item.title: 4th Circuit Vacates Lower Court Blockade On Trump’s Elimination Of DEI Grants
item.description: A federal appellate court handed President Trump a major victory on Friday by shutting down a lower court blockade on his efforts to eliminate racist ...

item.title: Democrats Hate Anyone Who Doesn’t Love Crime And Fraud As Much As They Do — Even Leftists
item.description: The first rule of Democrat Club is that you aren't supposed to mention the fraud and the grift.

item.title: A Viral Video Shows How The Left Coerces Fake Agreement
item.description: You’ve probably seen the video making the rounds today of the “Peak AWFL” meltdown in a Minneapolis yoga studio. You can see a bunch of obvious themes...

item.title: Michigan, Alabama Republicans Advance Bills To Cut Off Foreign Money In Elections
item.description: Michigan and Alabama Republicans took major steps this week to secure their states’ elections from foreign dark money. The GOP-controlled Michigan Hou...

item.title: Queer Lobby Reports 65% Drop In Fortune 500s Celebrating Corporate DEI
item.description: The number of Fortune 500 companies willing to publicly disclose their diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) practices has dropped 65 percent in the ...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...

item.title: Where AI Ends and Investment Judgment Begins
item.description: AI’s growing capabilities challenge traditional investment skill, shifting competitive advantage toward governance, process, and judgment. The post Wh...

item.title: Three Risks of Relying on the S&P 500 in Retirement Planning
item.description: Why S&P 500 reliance can undermine retirement outcomes, and how diversification, valuation discipline, and withdrawals reshape long-term portfolio ris...

item.title: Book Review: Principles of Bitcoin
item.description: A rigorous exploration of bitcoin reveals a new framework for understanding money beyond traditional financial models. The post Book Review: Principle...

item.title: AI Strategy After the LLM Boom: Maintain Sovereignty, Avoid Capture
item.description: Yann LeCun’s testimony reframed for investment leaders: why AI sovereignty, platform control, and LLM economics shape organizational risk. The post AI...
